content: 1¥t# Archon Project¥n2¥t¥n3¥tYou are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.¥n4¥t¥n5¥t## Priority Rule¥n6¥t¥n7¥tIf instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:¥n8¥t- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts¥n9¥t- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins¥n10¥t- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project¥n11¥t¥n12¥tWhen in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.¥n13¥t¥n14¥t## Skills¥n15¥t- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) ¥u2014 provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands¥n16¥t¥n17¥t## Tools¥n18¥t- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) ¥u2014 use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)¥n19¥t- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) ¥u2014 call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning¥n20¥t¥n21¥t## Key Files & Permissions¥n22¥t¥n23¥tAll state files are in `.archon/`:¥n24¥t¥n25¥t| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |¥n26¥t|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|¥n27¥t| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |¥n28¥t| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |¥n29¥t| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |¥n30¥t| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |¥n31¥t| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |¥n32¥t| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |¥n33¥t| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |¥n34¥t| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |¥n35¥t| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |¥n36¥t| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |¥n37¥t| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `¥¥lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `¥¥leanok`, `¥¥mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `¥¥lean{...}` corrections) | read |¥n38¥t| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |¥n39¥t| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |¥n40¥t¥n41¥t**Never write to `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` from the loop.** That file is only consumed by the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor execute` command run by the mathematician. The autonomous loop's plan agent must pass refactor directives **inline** in the Agent-tool prompt to the `refactor` subagent ¥u2014 never stage them in a file. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` flow; treat those as historical noise and prune them out when you rewrite those files.¥n42¥t¥n43¥t## Protected Declarations¥n44¥t¥n45¥t`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:¥n46¥t¥n47¥t- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.¥n48¥t- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.¥n49¥t- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. ¥n50¥t¥n51¥t## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary¥n52¥t¥n53¥tThe blueprint uses two active markers.¥n54¥t¥n55¥t- `¥¥leanok` ¥u2014 inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.¥n56¥t- `¥¥mathlibok` ¥u2014 inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.¥n57¥t- No marker ¥u2014 the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.¥n58¥t¥n59¥t**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters ¥u2014 they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `¥¥lean{...}` hints, but never markers.¥n60¥t¥n61¥t## User Interaction¥n62¥t¥n63¥tUsers provide hints in two places:¥n64¥t¥n65¥t- **Strategic hints** ¥u2192 `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.¥n66¥t- **File-specific hints** ¥u2192 `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.¥n67¥t¥n68¥t## Agent Roles¥n69¥t¥n70¥t### Plan Agent¥n71¥t- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions¥n72¥t- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` ¥u2014 incorporate hints, then clear them after acting¥n73¥t- Read `.archon/task_results/` ¥u2014 collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`¥n74¥t- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) via the Agent tool, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` ¥u00a7 ¥

for canonical ordering and per-subagent guidance. Pass each directive **inline** in the Agent-tool prompt.¥n75¥t- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.¥n76¥t- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round¥n77¥t- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `¥¥lean{...}` hints for the provers¥n78¥t- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself¥n79¥t¥n80¥t### Prover Agent¥n81¥t- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only ¥u2014 do not edit it)¥n82¥t- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:¥n83¥t  - autoformalize ¥u2192 `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`¥n84¥t  - prover ¥u2192 `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`¥n85¥t  - polish ¥u2192 `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`¥n86¥t- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`¥n87¥t- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned ¥u2014 **never edit another agent's file**¥n88¥t- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints¥n89¥t- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.¥n90¥t¥n91¥t### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)¥n92¥t¥n93¥tThese are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand via the Agent tool, passing the directive inline. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at ¥u2014 never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state ¥u2014 and writes a self-contained report.¥n94¥t¥n95¥t- **`refactor`** ¥u2014 read `.claude/agents/refactor.md` and `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.¥n96¥t- **`analogy`** ¥u2014 read `.claude/agents/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.¥n97¥t- **`challenger`** ¥u2014 read `.claude/agents/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.¥n98¥t¥n99¥tThe plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.¥n100¥t¥n101¥t### Review Agent¥n102¥t- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions¥n103¥t- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data¥n104¥t- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)¥n105¥t- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress¥n106¥t- **Update blueprint markers** (`¥¥leanok`, `¥¥mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results¥n107¥t- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1¥t# Review Agent ¥u2014 Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis¥n2¥t¥n3¥tYou are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) **update the blueprint chapter markers** (`¥¥leanok`, `¥¥mathlibok`) to reflect the verified state, and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.¥n4¥t¥n5¥t**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs. You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts ¥u2014 that is required for marker updates. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint files.**¥n6¥t¥n7¥t## Iteration number ¥u2014 canonical¥n8¥t¥n9¥tYour invocation prompt contains both `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. They are different counters and you must keep them straight:¥n10¥t¥n11¥t- `Archon iteration: NNN` is Archon's authoritative counter ¥u2014 matches `logs/iter-NNN/` and `archon[NNN/phase]` commit messages. Use it when you reference *the iteration this session belongs to* (in `summary.md` metadata) and *the next iteration* (when titling `recommendations.md`, e.g. ¥

). Do not invent your own counter. If older `recommendations.md` files use a different counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents ¥u2014 your new file uses the number from your prompt header.¥n12¥t- `Session number: M` is independent ¥u2014 it counts prover rounds only. Use it for the directory name (`session_M/`) and nothing else.¥n13¥t¥n14¥t## Step 1: Identify Context¥n15¥t¥n16¥t1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` ¥u2014 count existing session folders to determine the current session number.¥n17¥t2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.¥n18¥t3. Run `git diff HEAD‾1 --stat` to see what changed.¥n19¥t¥n20¥t## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)¥n21¥t¥n22¥t**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.¥n23¥t¥n24¥tThe file contains:¥n25¥t¥n26¥t- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: ¥

` ¥u2014 total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)¥n27¥t- Remaining lines: One event per tool call ¥u2014 edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds¥n28¥t¥n29¥t**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text ¥u2192 new_text).¥n30¥t**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.¥n31¥t**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.¥n32¥t**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.¥n33¥t¥n34¥tThis is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.¥n35¥t¥n36¥t## Step 3: Read Recent History¥n37¥t¥n38¥tIf previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.¥n39¥t¥n40¥t## Step 4: Write Proof Journal¥n41¥t¥n42¥tCreate the session folder and write two files:¥n43¥t¥n44¥t```bash¥n45¥tmkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>¥n46¥t```¥n47¥t¥n48¥t### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`¥n49¥t¥n50¥tMust include:¥n51¥t- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)¥n52¥t- For EACH target attempted:¥n53¥t  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point¥n54¥t  - What was learned from each failed attempt¥n55¥t  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas¥n56¥t- Key findings / proof patterns discovered¥n57¥t- Recommendations for next session¥n58¥t¥n59¥t### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`¥n60¥t¥n61¥tEach line MUST follow this JSON format ¥u2014 one entry per target theorem:¥n62¥t¥n63¥t```json¥n64¥t{¥n65¥t  ¥

¥n93¥t}¥n94¥t```¥n95¥t¥n96¥t**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:¥n97¥t- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded¥n98¥t- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)¥n99¥t- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as ¥

¥u2014 list each one¥n100¥t¥n101¥t### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`¥n102¥t¥n103¥tWrite concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:¥n104¥t- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized¥n105¥t- Which approaches showed promise but need more work¥n106¥t- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)¥n107¥t- Any reusable proof patterns discovered¥n108¥t¥n109¥t## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md¥n110¥t¥n111¥tUpdate (or create) `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`:¥n112¥t¥n113¥t```markdown¥n114¥t# Project Status¥n115¥t¥n116¥t## Overall Progress¥n117¥t- **Total sorry**: <N>¥n118¥t- **Solved this session**: <list with file + theorem>¥n119¥t- **Partial**: <list with progress summary>¥n120¥t- **Blocked**: <list with reasons>¥n121¥t- **Untouched**: <list>¥n122¥t¥n123¥t## Knowledge Base¥n124¥t### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)¥n125¥t- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>¥n126¥t¥n127¥t### Known Blockers (do not retry)¥n128¥t- <target>: <reason>¥n129¥t¥n130¥t## Last Updated¥n131¥t<ISO timestamp>¥n132¥t```¥n133¥t¥n134¥t## Step 6: Update Blueprint Markers¥n135¥t¥n136¥tYou are the **only** agent that writes blueprint markers. The prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) intentionally do not touch the blueprint ¥u2014 they only report what they did in `task_results/<file>.md`. Your job is to translate those reports into accurate markers.¥n137¥t¥n138¥tFor every declaration the prover session touched, decide which marker (if any) belongs on its blueprint block, then edit the relevant chapter file in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`.¥n139¥t¥n140¥t### Marker rules¥n141¥t¥n142¥t- `¥¥leanok` inside `¥¥begin{theorem}` / `¥¥begin{lemma}` / `¥¥begin{definition}` ¥u2014 the **statement** is formalized. Add this once the corresponding Lean declaration exists and the file compiles (even if the proof body is `sorry`).¥n143¥t- `¥¥leanok` inside `¥¥begin{proof}` ¥u2014 the **proof** is fully formalized. Add this only after verifying with `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lake env lean <file>` that the declaration has **no `sorry`, no `axiom`, no compilation errors**.¥n144¥t- `¥¥mathlibok` inside the statement block ¥u2014 the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export, alias, or direct reference. Add this when the prover's task result says the declaration is Mathlib-backed. No `¥¥leanok` on the proof block is expected for Mathlib-backed declarations.¥n145¥t- **No marker** ¥u2014 the block is unformalized or the informal statement did not translate cleanly. Leave unmarked and add a `% NOTE: <reason>` comment so the plan agent sees the obstacle.¥n146¥t¥n147¥t### What to verify before marking¥n148¥t¥n149¥t- **Statement `¥¥leanok`**: confirm the Lean declaration exists at the name given by `¥¥lean{...}` and the file compiles.¥n150¥t- **Proof `¥¥leanok`**: confirm the declaration has no `sorry`, no new `axiom`, no errors. If the prover's report claimed polish-complete but the file still has a `sorry`, do NOT add the proof `¥¥leanok` ¥u2014 trust verification over self-reports.¥n151¥t- **`¥¥mathlibok`**: confirm the Lean side references the Mathlib name (e.g. `def foo := Mathlib.bar` or a simple re-export) and does not itself contain a `sorry` or new definition requiring proof.¥n152¥t¥n153¥t### `¥¥lean{...}` macro maintenance¥n154¥t¥n155¥tIf a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `¥¥lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.¥n156¥t¥n157¥t### Record what you marked¥n158¥t¥n159¥tIn `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a ¥

section listing every marker change:¥n160¥t¥n161¥t```markdown¥n162¥t## Blueprint markers updated¥n163¥t- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `thm:wLocal_iff`: added `¥¥leanok` to statement (declaration exists, compiles)¥n164¥t- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `thm:wLocal_iff`: added `¥¥leanok` to proof (0 sorries, compiles, no axioms)¥n165¥t- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `¥¥mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)¥n166¥t- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: left unmarked ¥u2014 prover reported could-not-formalize¥n167¥t- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `¥¥notready`¥n168¥t```¥n169¥t¥n170¥t### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`¥n171¥t¥n172¥tWhile the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. ¥n173¥t¥n174¥t**Rules:**¥n175¥t- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).¥n176¥t- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.¥n177¥t¥n178¥t## Step 7: Self-Validation¥n179¥t¥n180¥tAfter writing all files, validate your output by checking:¥n181¥t- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line¥n182¥t- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`¥n183¥t- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`¥n184¥t- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`¥n185¥t- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries¥n186¥t- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps¥n187¥t- [ ] For every declaration solved or polished this session, the blueprint chapter has the correct marker (`¥¥leanok` on the proof block once the file compiles with no `sorry`; `¥¥mathlibok` if backed by Mathlib; nothing otherwise)¥n188¥t- [ ] Any `¥¥lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied¥n189¥t- [ ] No `¥¥notready` marker remains in any chapter¥n190¥t¥n191¥t## Permissions¥n192¥t¥n193¥tYou may write to:¥n194¥t- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)¥n195¥t- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`¥n196¥t- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` ¥u2014 markers (`¥¥leanok`, `¥¥mathlibok`), `¥¥lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose ¥u2014 that is the plan agent's surface.¥n197¥t¥n198¥tYou must NOT write to:¥n199¥t- Any `.lean` files¥n200¥t- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (plan agent's responsibility)¥n201¥t- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)¥n202¥t- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) ¥u2014 only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.¥n203¥t
